name: Besurta - Renclusa - Aiguallut Circular Trail
name_original: Besurta - Aigualluts - Renclusa – circular por el GR.11
description: Hike the moderate 4.4-mile Besurta - Aigualluts - Renclusa circular trail in Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, featuring unique geological forma
tips: If instead of standing at the waterfall you advance two more minutes you will have a spectacular photo with the Aneto glacier.
It is a well-known tourist attraction. The site is spectacular and the history of this sinkhole makes it even more special. The best time to visit is in spring, after the ice melts.

This mesmerizing plain is an ancient filled lake from the immense glacier that occupied the entire valley thousands of years ago. A special place with a particular sound, as if silence occupied the space in a tangible way.

High mountain refuge located at more than 2,000 m that serves as a base camp for the classic ascents of Maladeta and Aneto, among others.

More information: https://www.alberguesyrefugios.com/larenclusa/

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Besurta - Aigualluts - Renclusa circular hike?
faqs.answer: The hike is rated as moderate and can take approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes to 5 hours to complete, depending on your fitness level and pace. The total duration for this specific route is around 3 hours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied landscape, characteristic of the Pyrenean environment. You'll traverse green meadows where the Ésera river meanders, as well as rocky outcrops and some stony sections. While largely accessible, some parts involve a steady ascent, particularly on the way to the Renclusa Mountain Hut.

faqs.question: What are the main natural highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty. Key highlights include the stunning Forau de Aiguallut, a unique karstic sinkhole where the Ésera river disappears underground. You'll also encounter the picturesque Aiguallut waterfall, the expansive Plan d'Aigualluts, and the scenic Aigualluts Valley. The Renclusa Mountain Hut itself is a notable landmark.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from La Besurta. Parking is available at La Besurta, but during peak season, access might be restricted, and you may need to use a shuttle service from further down the valley. It's advisable to check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Besurta - Aigualluts - Renclusa circular trail?
faqs.answer: The summer and autumn months are generally recommended to fully enjoy the spectacular scenery without major complications, provided there is no significant snow. Spring, after the ice melts, is also an excellent time to visit the Forau de Aiguallut.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this hike requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While some sections are less demanding, others involve steady ascents and stony paths. It might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children, but active families with hiking experience could enjoy it.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Besurta - Aigualluts - Renclusa circular trail?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always follow park regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific entrance fees or permits required to hike this trail within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park for day visits. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements, especially for overnight stays or certain activities.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is part of the GR.11, a long-distance trail that traverses the Spanish Pyrenees. The route also passes through sections of the 'Variante Plan de Senarta - Refugio cap de Llauset' and 'Étape 22'.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: The Renclusa Mountain Hut is located directly on the route. It serves as a refuge and a common starting point for ascents to Aneto, offering accommodation and basic services. It's advisable to book in advance if you plan to stay overnight.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail offers magnificent views of the valley and surrounding peaks, including the Maladeta massif and Aneto, the highest peak in the Pyrenees. You'll be treated to breathtaking vistas of the high Pyrenees throughout the journey.
